mission.title: Install and Configure Openyurt on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenYurt extends your native Kubernetes to edge environments, enabling seamless management of edge applications as if they were running in the cloud. Installing OpenYurt allows you to address challenges in cloud-edge orchestration, such as unreliable networking and edge device management.

steps.title: Create OpenYurt Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for OpenYurt to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Install OpenYurt Core Components
steps.description: Apply the core components of OpenYurt using the manifests from the GitHub repository.

steps.title: Install Yurt Hub
steps.description: Apply the Yurt Hub manifest to enable communication between the cloud and edge nodes.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description: Check that the OpenYurt pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the yurt-iot-dock pods running in the 'openyurt' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. Additionally, resource limits will be configured for optimal performance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall OpenYurt
uninstall.description: Remove the OpenYurt components from your cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenYurt
upgrade.description: To upgrade OpenYurt, apply the new manifests for the desired version.

troubleshooting.title: Check Pod Status
troubleshooting.description: If pods are not running, check their status and logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Verify Node Registration
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that edge nodes are properly registered with the control plane.

troubleshooting.title: Inspect Events
troubleshooting.description: Check for any events that might indicate issues with the OpenYurt components.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed on your local machine.
